newsSince: One incremental governance item since June 9: on June 15 the board withdrew agenda items 14-16 from the 2026 AGM amid a boardroom reset - Vice Chairman Tomas Duffy and founders Erik Fallstrom and Keith Halsey resigned, with Joakim Nideborn and Helena Svancar joining. The core thesis driver, the June 2 GlobalFoundries collaboration (Sivers laser arrays into GF silicon-photonics / SCALE CPO/LPO reference designs), predates June 9. Stock +14.4% since June 9.

sources.label: Sivers & GlobalFoundries advance AI data-center optics (PR Newswire)
Sivers withdraws AGM agenda items 14-16 (PR Newswire)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO/photonics alpha lens the GF laser-array-into-SCALE collaboration is exactly the thesis - small-cap, high-leverage CPO exposure. But the GF deal predates June 9 and the only new item, the June 15 AGM withdrawal and board resignations (founders + vice-chair out), is a governance flag that re-opens execution/validation risk on a ~1,945% YTD single-bundle name. Hold with a small negative tilt.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: GlobalFoundries silicon-photonics/SCALE CPO/LPO reference-design wins for Sivers laser arrays.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Board turmoil/governance, single-bundle validation risk, extreme YTD re-rate.
verdicts.bull: GF partnership puts Sivers lasers into a leading CPO platform - asymmetric optical-bottleneck upside for a $3B-cap name.
verdicts.neutral: Right thesis, but a fresh board reset and parabolic move offset the GF optionality.
verdicts.bear: Founder/vice-chair exits and withdrawn AGM items raise governance risk on a thinly-validated, hyper-extended stock.
